county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Delaware. Ohio Wesleyan University (1842) archives hold local materials. Rutherford B. Hayes born here (1822). Located in Congress Lands. Delaware County Historical Society maintains family files. Olentangy River valley settlement patterns. Rapidly growing county (Columbus suburbs) — modern records extensive but early records sparse as county was frontier. Check Franklin County for pre-1808 records. Stratford Ecological Center area.

source_profiles.repository: Delaware County Recorder, Delaware
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1808. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Ohio deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.repository: Delaware County Probate Court, Delaware
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and returns from 1808. Ohio Probate Court has jurisdiction over marriages. Applications include ages, birthplaces, and parents' names (post-1860s details improve).
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved at Probate Court

source_profiles.source_name: Delaware County Will and Probate Records
source_profiles.repository: Delaware County Probate Court, Delaware
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, guardianships, and estate inventories from 1808. Ohio Probate Court handles all estate matters. Inventories often list personal property in detail.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved

source_profiles.source_name: Delaware County Court of Common Pleas Records
source_profiles.repository: Delaware County Clerk of Courts, Delaware
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Civil and criminal case files, naturalization records (pre-1906), and judgments from 1808. Ohio Court of Common Pleas is the general trial court. Early records include debt cases, property disputes, and bastardy cases that identify family relationships.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Court records generally well-preserved at county level

source_profiles.repository: Delaware County Auditor, Delaware / Ohio History Connection
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Tax duplicates list property owners, land descriptions, and property values. Ohio county auditor records from 1808. Early tax lists serve as census substitutes before 1820.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Some tax duplicates at Ohio History Connection on microfilm

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Sandusky. Created 1838 from Huron and Sandusky counties — check both for earlier records. Lake Erie port city; Sandusky was a major point on the Underground Railroad route to Canada via boat. Cedar Point area history. Johnson's Island Civil War prison records at Ohio History Connection — Union and Confederate prisoner records. Erie County Historical Society. German, Irish, and Italian immigrant communities. Firelands (Sufferers' Lands) area — Connecticut Western Reserve fire sufferers land grants.

source_profiles.repository: Erie County Recorder, Sandusky
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1838. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Ohio deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.repository: Erie County Probate Court, Sandusky
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and returns from 1838. Ohio Probate Court has jurisdiction over marriages. Applications include ages, birthplaces, and parents' names (post-1860s details improve).
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved at Probate Court

source_profiles.source_name: Erie County Will and Probate Records
source_profiles.repository: Erie County Probate Court, Sandusky
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, guardianships, and estate inventories from 1838. Ohio Probate Court handles all estate matters. Inventories often list personal property in detail.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved

source_profiles.source_name: Connecticut Western Reserve Land Records — Erie County
source_profiles.repository: Western Reserve Historical Society, Cleveland / CT State Library
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Connecticut ceded its Western Reserve to the federal government in 1800, but settlers arrived from 1796. Connecticut Land Company records document original purchasers and settlers. Check Connecticut state records for settler origins.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved at WRHS in Cleveland; Connecticut State Library also has copies

source_profiles.repository: Connecticut State Library / Firelands Historical Society, Norwalk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: The Firelands were granted by Connecticut to residents whose property was burned by the British during the Revolutionary War. Claims document the original Connecticut property owners and their heirs who received Ohio lands.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Published lists of fire sufferers exist; original records at CT State Library

source_profiles.repository: Erie County Clerk of Courts, Sandusky / NARA Chicago
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Declarations of intention, petitions for naturalization, and certificates. Pre-1906 records at county Court of Common Pleas. Post-1906 copies at NARA. Ohio had massive European immigration — German, Irish, Welsh, English, Italian, Hungarian, Slovak, Polish, Czech, and other groups.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Many Ohio naturalization records indexed on FamilySearch and Ancestry

source_profiles.source_name: Underground Railroad and Abolitionist Records — Erie County
source_profiles.repository: Ohio History Connection / National Underground Railroad Freedom Center
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Ohio was a free state under the Northwest Ordinance (1787). Court records include fugitive slave cases, abolitionist society minutes, and related legal proceedings. Anti-slavery newspapers published in Ohio are valuable genealogical sources.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Scattered across local historical societies and Ohio History Connection

source_profiles.repository: Ohio History Connection / NARA
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Ohio provided approximately 320,000 soldiers to the Union Army — third most of any state. Roster Commission records, enlistment papers, discharge records, and pension files. Ohio History Connection holds the Ohio Roster Commission records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Ohio Roster Commission published 'Official Roster of the Soldiers of the State of Ohio in the War of the Rebellion' (12 volumes)

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Lancaster. One of the earliest Ohio counties (1800). Located in Congress Lands. Lancaster is one of Ohio's oldest cities; General William Tecumseh Sherman and General Philip Sheridan were born here. Fairfield County Heritage Association. Strong German heritage — 'Fairfield' means 'beautiful field' in German (Schoenfeld). Hocking Canal records. Extensive early deed records from 1800. Ohio University Lancaster campus archives.

source_profiles.repository: Fairfield County Recorder, Lancaster
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1800. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Ohio deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.repository: Fairfield County Probate Court, Lancaster
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and returns from 1800. Ohio Probate Court has jurisdiction over marriages. Applications include ages, birthplaces, and parents' names (post-1860s details improve).
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved at Probate Court

source_profiles.source_name: Fairfield County Will and Probate Records
source_profiles.repository: Fairfield County Probate Court, Lancaster
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, guardianships, and estate inventories from 1800. Ohio Probate Court handles all estate matters. Inventories often list personal property in detail.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved

source_profiles.source_name: Fairfield County Court of Common Pleas Records
source_profiles.repository: Fairfield County Clerk of Courts, Lancaster
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Civil and criminal case files, naturalization records (pre-1906), and judgments from 1800. Ohio Court of Common Pleas is the general trial court. Early records include debt cases, property disputes, and bastardy cases that identify family relationships.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Court records generally well-preserved at county level

source_profiles.repository: Fairfield County Auditor, Lancaster / Ohio History Connection
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Tax duplicates list property owners, land descriptions, and property values. Ohio county auditor records from 1800. Early tax lists serve as census substitutes before 1820.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Some tax duplicates at Ohio History Connection on microfilm

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Washington Court House. Located in the Virginia Military District — VA bounty land warrants critical for early land research. County seat name reflects the court that administered VA military warrants. Paint Creek and Deer Creek valleys — early Shawnee territory. Fayette County Historical Society. Underground Railroad routes. Agricultural county with strong cattle-raising heritage — livestock brand records. Check Ross and Highland for pre-1810 records.

source_profiles.repository: Fayette County Recorder, Washington Court House
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1810. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Ohio deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.repository: Fayette County Probate Court, Washington Court House
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and returns from 1810. Ohio Probate Court has jurisdiction over marriages. Applications include ages, birthplaces, and parents' names (post-1860s details improve).
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved at Probate Court

source_profiles.source_name: Fayette County Will and Probate Records
source_profiles.repository: Fayette County Probate Court, Washington Court House
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, guardianships, and estate inventories from 1810. Ohio Probate Court handles all estate matters. Inventories often list personal property in detail.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved

source_profiles.source_name: Fayette County Court of Common Pleas Records
source_profiles.repository: Fayette County Clerk of Courts, Washington Court House
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Civil and criminal case files, naturalization records (pre-1906), and judgments from 1810. Ohio Court of Common Pleas is the general trial court. Early records include debt cases, property disputes, and bastardy cases that identify family relationships.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Court records generally well-preserved at county level

source_profiles.repository: Fayette County Auditor, Washington Court House / Ohio History Connection
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Tax duplicates list property owners, land descriptions, and property values. Ohio county auditor records from 1810. Early tax lists serve as census substitutes before 1820.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Some tax duplicates at Ohio History Connection on microfilm

source_profiles.source_name: Virginia Military District Land Warrants — Fayette County
source_profiles.repository: Ohio History Connection / Library of Virginia / BLM-GLO
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Virginia military bounty land warrants issued to Virginia Revolutionary War veterans. The Virginia Military District covered south-central Ohio between the Scioto and Little Miami Rivers. Warrants often bought and sold — chain of ownership can trace families. BLM-GLO has original federal patents.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Records survive at multiple repositories — Ohio History Connection, Library of Virginia, and NARA

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Columbus. State capital since 1816 — Ohio History Connection (primary state repository) is here. Columbus Metropolitan Library has extensive genealogy collection. Franklin County Probate Court holds records from 1803. Ohio State University archives. German Village historic neighborhood — German immigrant records extensive. Camp Chase Civil War prison cemetery and records. Columbus served as a major Union Army staging area. Franklin County Genealogical Society. City directories from 1843.

source_profiles.repository: Franklin County Recorder, Columbus
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1803. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Ohio deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and returns from 1803. Ohio Probate Court has jurisdiction over marriages. Applications include ages, birthplaces, and parents' names (post-1860s details improve).

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Virginia military bounty land warrants issued to Virginia Revolutionary War veterans. The Virginia Military District covered south-central Ohio between the Scioto and Little Miami Rivers. Warrants often bought and sold — chain of ownership can trace families. BLM-GLO has original federal patents.

source_profiles.repository: Franklin County Clerk of Courts, Columbus / NARA Chicago
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Declarations of intention, petitions for naturalization, and certificates. Pre-1906 records at county Court of Common Pleas. Post-1906 copies at NARA. Ohio had massive European immigration — German, Irish, Welsh, English, Italian, Hungarian, Slovak, Polish, Czech, and other groups.

source_profiles.source_name: Underground Railroad and Abolitionist Records — Franklin County
source_profiles.repository: Ohio History Connection / National Underground Railroad Freedom Center
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Ohio was a free state under the Northwest Ordinance (1787). Court records include fugitive slave cases, abolitionist society minutes, and related legal proceedings. Anti-slavery newspapers published in Ohio are valuable genealogical sources.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Scattered across local historical societies and Ohio History Connection

source_profiles.repository: Ohio History Connection, Columbus
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Canal construction records, land purchases, worker rolls, and canal-related property records. The Ohio & Erie Canal (Cleveland to Portsmouth) and Miami & Erie Canal (Cincinnati to Toledo) transformed Ohio's economy and brought many immigrants.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Canal Commission records at Ohio History Connection

source_profiles.repository: Ohio History Connection / NARA
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Ohio provided approximately 320,000 soldiers to the Union Army — third most of any state. Roster Commission records, enlistment papers, discharge records, and pension files. Ohio History Connection holds the Ohio Roster Commission records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Ohio Roster Commission published 'Official Roster of the Soldiers of the State of Ohio in the War of the Rebellion' (12 volumes)

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Wauseon. Created 1850 from Lucas, Henry, and Williams — check all three parent counties for pre-1850 records. Last organized county in NW Ohio; late settlement due to Black Swamp. Fulton County Historical Society in Wauseon. Strong German heritage — Archbold and surrounding communities. Mennonite and Amish settlement records. Sauder Village documents rural heritage. Congress Lands survey system.

source_profiles.repository: Fulton County Recorder, Wauseon
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1850. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Ohio deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.repository: Fulton County Probate Court, Wauseon
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and returns from 1850. Ohio Probate Court has jurisdiction over marriages. Applications include ages, birthplaces, and parents' names (post-1860s details improve).
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved at Probate Court

source_profiles.source_name: Fulton County Will and Probate Records
source_profiles.repository: Fulton County Probate Court, Wauseon
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, guardianships, and estate inventories from 1850. Ohio Probate Court handles all estate matters. Inventories often list personal property in detail.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved

source_profiles.source_name: Amish and Mennonite Church Records — Fulton County
source_profiles.repository: Heritage Historical Library, Aylmer ON / Mennonite Historical Library, Goshen IN / Local church records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Amish church district records, family group sheets, Budget newspaper correspondence, and Mennonite church records. Ohio has the largest Amish population in the world. Records may be in German/Pennsylvanian Dutch. Die Botschaft and The Budget newspapers document Amish community events.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Many Amish records remain in private family collections; Heritage Historical Library has compiled indexes

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Gallipolis. French settlement (1790) — Gallipolis was founded by the Scioto Company (French emigrants). Check French genealogical sources for Gallipolis founders. Ohio Company Purchase lands. Gallia County Historical/Genealogical Society. Bob Evans Farms heritage. Ohio River commerce records. Our House Tavern (1819) is a state memorial — early tavern license records. Coal mining community in later period. Welsh immigrant community in certain townships.

source_profiles.repository: Gallia County Recorder, Gallipolis
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1803. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Ohio deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and returns from 1803. Ohio Probate Court has jurisdiction over marriages. Applications include ages, birthplaces, and parents' names (post-1860s details improve).

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Civil and criminal case files, naturalization records (pre-1906), and judgments from 1803. Ohio Court of Common Pleas is the general trial court. Early records include debt cases, property disputes, and bastardy cases that identify family relationships.

source_profiles.source_name: Ohio Company of Associates Land Records — Gallia County
source_profiles.repository: Marietta College Archives / Ohio History Connection
source_profiles.coverage_notes: The Ohio Company of Associates (organized 1786 in Massachusetts) purchased 1.5 million acres in SE Ohio. Records document original purchasers, many from New England. Marietta was the first permanent settlement.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved at Marietta College Special Collections and Ohio History Connection

source_profiles.source_name: Underground Railroad and Abolitionist Records — Gallia County
source_profiles.repository: Ohio History Connection / National Underground Railroad Freedom Center
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Ohio was a free state under the Northwest Ordinance (1787). Court records include fugitive slave cases, abolitionist society minutes, and related legal proceedings. Anti-slavery newspapers published in Ohio are valuable genealogical sources.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Scattered across local historical societies and Ohio History Connection

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Chardon. Western Reserve county — early settlers from Connecticut and Vermont. Amish and Mennonite communities in Burton-Middlefield area — church records essential. Maple syrup industry heritage — agricultural records. Geauga County Historical Society in Burton. Connecticut Land Company records at WRHS, Cleveland. Century Village Museum documents early settlement. Very early county (1805) with good record survival.

source_profiles.repository: Geauga County Recorder, Chardon
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1805. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Ohio deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Xenia. Located partly in Virginia Military District. Wright-Patterson Air Force Base (originally Wright Field) — military personnel records. Wright brothers heritage in Dayton connects across county lines. Wilberforce University (1856) and Central State University — historically Black institutions with important records. Xenia was devastated by the 1974 tornado — some local records and personal papers damaged or lost. Greene County Historical Society. Strong Quaker heritage in Xenia area.

source_profiles.source_name: Society of Friends (Quaker) Meeting Records — Greene County
source_profiles.repository: Wilmington College Quaker Heritage Center / Ohio History Connection
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Monthly meeting records include births, marriages, deaths, removals (transfers), and disownments. Quakers kept meticulous records. Ohio Yearly Meeting and Indiana Yearly Meeting records at Wilmington College.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Exceptionally well-preserved; many microfilmed and indexed

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Cambridge. Named for Guernsey in the English Channel Islands — English settler heritage. National Road (Route 40) corridor; tavern and stagecoach records. Cambridge glass factory records document worker families. Salt Fork State Park area. Guernsey County Genealogical Society. Coal mining records in eastern townships. Dickens Victorian Village reflects English heritage. Located in Congress Lands. Check Belmont and Muskingum for pre-1810 records.
